name: Bread and Puppet Theater
description: Bread and Puppet Theater is a renowned political puppet theater company known for its giant papier-mâché puppets used in parades, protests, and performances. Based in Glover, Vermont with performances in Cambridge, MA and internationally.

name: New York Village Halloween Parade
description: America's largest Halloween parade, held annually on October 31 in Greenwich Village, NYC. Known for its giant skeleton and monster puppets created by the Processional Arts Workshop.

name: Royal de Luxe Giant Puppet Spectacles
description: Royal de Luxe is a French street theater company famous for giant mechanical puppet spectacles performed in cities worldwide, including Liverpool, London, and Nantes. Known for 'The Sultan's Elephant' and 'The Giants of Nantes.'

name: Little Amal (The Walk)
description: Little Amal is a 3.5-meter-tall giant puppet of a Syrian refugee girl that tours cities worldwide to raise awareness of refugee children. Created by the Good Chance Theatre, she walks on stilts with an internal puppeteer controlling her head.

name: Dussehra Festival (Giant Effigies)
description: The Dussehra festival in India features giant puppet effigies of Ravana, Kumbhakarna, and Meghnath that are burned at the end of the festival to celebrate the victory of good over evil. Giant puppets controlled by lines are used in parades.

name: The Man Engine (Cornwall Mining Puppet)
description: The Man Engine is a 40-ton mechanical puppet of a Cornish miner that toured south-west England in 2016 to highlight mining heritage. Created to mark the 150th anniversary of a mining disaster.
